nativeRange.habitat: Regionally collected from the Lake Sulawesi area in Indonesia; specific micro-habitat is not documented in trade sources, but the genus Ammannia is broadly associated with shallow wet/marshy margins of lakes, ponds, and slow-moving freshwater.

decorPreferences: high light to develop orange/pink/red coloration
stable parameters — sensitive to swings during emersed-to-submersed transition

summary: A regionally collected red-orange-pink stem plant from the Lake Sulawesi area of Indonesia; generally considered a more compact, slightly less demanding alternative to its sister Ammannia gracilis, prized in the hobby for warm orange-to-pink tones under strong light and CO2.
careNotes: Ammannia sp. 'Sulawesi' is a regionally collected stem plant from the Lake Sulawesi area of Indonesia and has no formal species description yet — it is documented as 'sp.' (genus-only) in the hobby trade and is taxonomically a sister to Ammannia gracilis (fw-plant-053, tropical West Africa) and Ammannia senegalensis (fw-plant-088). Compared with A. gracilis, this Sulawesi form is described by retailers as more compact (commonly 10-20+ cm tall vs A. gracilis's 20-50 cm), with a green-orange-pink-red colour palette rather than the deeper copper-red of gracilis, and is generally considered slightly less demanding although still recommended for hobbyists with stable, mature, fertilized tanks. Sources broadly agree on a temperature range of roughly 22-28 °C (72-82 °F), pH 6.0-7.5, and soft-to-moderately-hard water (gH ~2-12, kH ~2-8). To bring out the characteristic orange-pink coloration, the plant generally needs high light (~30-50 PAR class), pressurized CO2, and consistent iron + macro/micro fertilization; under medium light it tends to stay greener and less vibrant. It prefers a nutrient-rich substrate (aquasoil or root tabs) and regular trimming, which encourages a bushier, compact mid-to-background shape. Like other Ammannia, it can be sensitive to parameter swings during the emersed-to-submersed transition (especially tissue-culture stems), so stable water chemistry and gradual acclimation are commonly advised. Source note: the manifest's primary URL (https://buceplant.com/products/ammania-sp-sulawesi) returned 404 during research; the primary-source role was swapped to Aqua Lab Aquaria, with Ultum Nature Systems and Aquatic Plant Man as additional cross-checks, and Wikipedia used only for genus-level taxonomy (Lythraceae, Myrtales).
breedingNotes: Propagated by stem cuttings: snip the upper portion of a healthy stem (about 5-10 cm with several leaves) and replant into the substrate. Trimming established stems encourages lateral side-shoot growth and a bushier compact form. Seed/flower-based propagation is not documented in hobby sources for this regional form.

primary.name: Aqua Lab Aquaria
primary.notes: Promoted to primary because the manifest-supplied Buce Plant URL (https://buceplant.com/products/ammania-sp-sulawesi) returned HTTP 404. Used for adult height (4-8+ in / 10-20+ cm), light (high), CO2 (beneficial/recommended), growth rate (moderate), placement (midground/background), difficulty (intermediate), origin (Lake Sulawesi, Indonesia), and colour (green/red, paling to orange).

additional.name: Ultum Nature Systems
additional.notes: Cross-checked origin (Indonesia), difficulty (intermediate; not overly difficult but recommended for experienced hobbyists with mature, balanced tanks), placement (midground-to-background), lighting (moderate-to-high), CO2 (required), nutrient dosing needed, and orange-green-pink colour palette.

additional.name: Buce Plant (via web search snippet)
additional.notes: Manifest's intended primary; direct fetch returned 404. Content extracted via web-search snippet only: confirms temperature 72-82 °F (22-28 °C), pH 6.0-7.5, soft-to-moderate hardness, CO2 injection + moderate-to-high lighting + quality nutrient dosing, regular trimming for compact bushy shape, propagation by stem cuttings, and difficulty 'not particularly difficult but recommended for those experienced with finicky stems'. Origin Lake Sulawesi, Indonesia.

additional.name: Aquatic Plant Man
additional.notes: Cross-checked colour (red hue with orange around the leaf), midground placement, and 'fairly easy' difficulty (tilts slightly lower than other sources, contributing to the averaged 'intermediate' rating).

additional.name: Wikipedia — Ammannia
additional.notes: Genus-level taxonomy only (family Lythraceae, order Myrtales, subfamily Lythroideae). Wikipedia does not mention the Sulawesi regional form; this species has no formal description, hence the 'Ammannia sp.' name.
